mentions.note: Digital finance platform down 30% from $26; at current valuation with strong growth rate might be worth reconsidering but wait for more clarity.

Federal-chartered bank with fortress fundamentals: Q4 showed 37% revenue growth, 160% earnings growth, 35% membership growth to 13.7M. FY2026 guidance: 30% member growth, 52% EBITDA growth, 54% EPS growth. Trading at 31x 2026E with PEG of 6.0—reasonable for 50%+ growth. AI cannot disrupt banking.
Recommended as twofer with Affirm; fintech player with real business fundamentals.

Caller Dustin: company posting first $1B+ quarter, record revenue, strong growth, 9 straight quarters GAAP profitable; CEO bought shares twice in March. Cramer: 'I'm a believer in SoFi'; bulls right despite controversy; knows CEO Anthony Noto since 1998, trusts his character.
At $15 (down from $30, $50 highs); didn't like at higher price; 'cut and run'—lukewarm hold; wait for clarity.

41% revenue growth, 18 consecutive quarters beating golden rule of 40; no guidance raise due to zero Fed rate cuts priced in; strong non-lending revenue growth ($390M) and Trump account opportunity; underlying trends robust despite stock selloff.
Time to buy at current 18 level after rejection at 28 and pullback from highs. Fintech thesis intact; continue to believe this level is attractive entry.
Stock stuck around 18.5 level; great level to get involved; CEO Anthony Noto building younger customer base; more service company than bank; Cramer likes strategy.
